title: agrostopoa barclayae
intro: Agrostopoa barclayae is a flowering plant species in the grass family Poaceae. It is a perennial grass native to New Zealand, and its leaves are used in traditional medicine. The medicinal properties of Agrostopoa barclayae include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally used to treat wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the leaves can be made into a tea or tincture to utilize its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting immune system function.
